system.description: Pick an edge you can see or feel (a rooftop, a doorway, a table, a brick, a paper). Within one minute, pick another and roll [quality].
system.synopsis: Conceptually join two or more edges

outcomes.success: you Stitch them, connecting the two edges. Visually, they remain in their original place, but spatially they become directly connected. Something passing, moving, or stepping from one edge will move directly on to the other, regardless of how far apart they are or whether anything is between them.
outcomes.every3rd3: you can Stitch more of these edges without breaking the first Stitch, potentially creating a chain or loop. If more than one hour passes since your last Stitch, you lose these remaining Stitches.
outcomes.failure: you accidentally Stitch yourself to the first edge, and any time you cross through a threshold you find yourself back at the first edge. You can forcibly rip this Stitch but will suffer from Burnout on all rolls for the rest of the day.

improvement.question: When I don't understand the instructions, I am more likely to...
